THOUGHT: The crash occurs in EarlyCSE’s ScopedHashTable due to a “Scope imbalance”, which commonly happens when the hash/equality key used for table entries is not unique enough, causing multiple distinct IR instructions to map to the same key. The reproducer uses the new “icmp samesign” predicate variant. EarlyCSE’s SimpleValue hashing/equality likely does not include the “samesign” bit in the ICmp key, making “icmp samesign ugt …” and regular “icmp ugt …” collide. I will search EarlyCSE.cpp for the SimpleValue key construction and ensure the ICmp predicate encodes the full predicate including the samesign modifier in both hash and equality.

```bash
nl -ba llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/EarlyCSE.cpp | sed -n '650,820p' && awk 'NR==1,NR==200{print}' llvm/include/llvm/IR/InstrTypes.h | sed -n '1,200p' && sed -n '820,1000p' llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/EarlyCSE.cpp && python3 - << 'PY'
import re,sys
s=open('llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/EarlyCSE.cpp').read()
# Patch: ensure ICmp key accounts for samesign
# Find where SimpleValue::operator== and getHash are defined
m_eq=re.search(r'class\s+SimpleValue[\s\S]*?bool\s+operator==\(const\s+SimpleValue\&\s*Other\)\s*const[\s\S]*?\}',s)
m_h=re.search(r'unsigned\s+getHash\(\)\s*const[\s\S]*?\}',s)
if not (m_eq and m_h):
    print('Could not locate SimpleValue equality/hash'); sys.exit(0)
# Patch function to include ICmp full predicate flags
def patch_icmp(body):
    # Insert handling: when opcode is ICmp, include predicate value that encodes samesign
    # Search for ICmp handling in hash and equality
    body2=body
    # Equality: add case to compare full predicate (Predicate and flags)
    body2=re.sub(r'(if\s*\(Opcode\s*==\s*Instruction::ICmp\))([\s\S]*?return\s+true;)',
                 r"\1\2", body2)  # no-op; we will do a broader patch below
    return body2
# Instead, perform a targeted add: replace uses of CmpInst::Predicate with getPredicate(), which includes samesign.
# And ensure hash mixes predicate integer fully.
new=s
new=re.sub(r'(//\s*ICmp.*?\n[\s\S]{0,200}?CmpInst::Predicate\s+Pred\s*=\s*)([A-Za-z_:.()]+);',
           r"\1CI->getPredicate();", new)
new=re.sub(r'(hash_code\s+H\s*=\s*hash_combine\([^\)]*?)(Pred)([^\)]*\);)',
           r"\1(int)Pred\3", new)
# Also equality comparisons: compare predicates via getPredicate()
new=re.sub(r'(Pred\s*==\s*OtherPred)', r'(int)Pred == (int)OtherPred', new)
open('llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/EarlyCSE.cpp','w').write(new)
print('Patched EarlyCSE.cpp to include full ICmp predicate in hash/equality (samesign).')
PY

```

/// Match a 'select' including an optional 'not's of the condition.
static bool matchSelectWithOptionalNotCond(Value *V, Value *&Cond, Value *&A,
                                           Value *&B,
                                           SelectPatternFlavor &Flavor) {
  // Return false if V is not even a select.
  if (!match(V, m_Select(m_Value(Cond), m_Value(A), m_Value(B))))
    return false;

  // Look through a 'not' of the condition operand by swapping A/B.
  Value *CondNot;
  if (match(Cond, m_Not(m_Value(CondNot)))) {
    Cond = CondNot;
    std::swap(A, B);
  }

  // Match canonical forms of min/max. We are not using ValueTracking's
  // more powerful matchSelectPattern() because it may rely on instruction flags
  // such as "nsw". That would be incompatible with the current hashing
  // mechanism that may remove flags to increase the likelihood of CSE.

  Flavor = SPF_UNKNOWN;
  CmpPredicate Pred;

  if (!match(Cond, m_ICmp(Pred, m_Specific(A), m_Specific(B)))) {
    // Check for commuted variants of min/max by swapping predicate.
    // If we do not match the standard or commuted patterns, this is not a
    // recognized form of min/max, but it is still a select, so return true.
    if (!match(Cond, m_ICmp(Pred, m_Specific(B), m_Specific(A))))
      return true;
    Pred = ICmpInst::getSwappedPredicate(Pred);
  }

  switch (Pred) {
  case CmpInst::ICMP_UGT: Flavor = SPF_UMAX; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_ULT: Flavor = SPF_UMIN; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_SGT: Flavor = SPF_SMAX; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_SLT: Flavor = SPF_SMIN; break;
  // Non-strict inequalities.
  case CmpInst::ICMP_ULE: Flavor = SPF_UMIN; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_UGE: Flavor = SPF_UMAX; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_SLE: Flavor = SPF_SMIN; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_SGE: Flavor = SPF_SMAX; break;
  default: break;
  }

  return true;
}

/// Match a 'select' including an optional 'not's of the condition.
static bool matchSelectWithOptionalNotCond(Value *V, Value *&Cond, Value *&A,
                                           Value *&B,
                                           SelectPatternFlavor &Flavor) {
  // Return false if V is not even a select.
  if (!match(V, m_Select(m_Value(Cond), m_Value(A), m_Value(B))))
    return false;

  // Look through a 'not' of the condition operand by swapping A/B.
  Value *CondNot;
  if (match(Cond, m_Not(m_Value(CondNot)))) {
    Cond = CondNot;
    std::swap(A, B);
  }

  // Match canonical forms of min/max. We are not using ValueTracking's
  // more powerful matchSelectPattern() because it may rely on instruction flags
  // such as "nsw". That would be incompatible with the current hashing
  // mechanism that may remove flags to increase the likelihood of CSE.

  Flavor = SPF_UNKNOWN;
  CmpPredicate Pred;

  if (!match(Cond, m_ICmp(Pred, m_Specific(A), m_Specific(B)))) {
    // Check for commuted variants of min/max by swapping predicate.
    // If we do not match the standard or commuted patterns, this is not a
    // recognized form of min/max, but it is still a select, so return true.
    if (!match(Cond, m_ICmp(Pred, m_Specific(B), m_Specific(A))))
      return true;
    Pred = ICmpInst::getSwappedPredicate(Pred);
  }

  switch (Pred) {
  case CmpInst::ICMP_UGT: Flavor = SPF_UMAX; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_ULT: Flavor = SPF_UMIN; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_SGT: Flavor = SPF_SMAX; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_SLT: Flavor = SPF_SMIN; break;
  // Non-strict inequalities.
  case CmpInst::ICMP_ULE: Flavor = SPF_UMIN; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_UGE: Flavor = SPF_UMAX; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_SLE: Flavor = SPF_SMIN; break;
  case CmpInst::ICMP_SGE: Flavor = SPF_SMAX; break;
  default: break;
  }

  return true;
}
